top_level_architecture.summary: c1v is an AI-native PRD + system-design tool. The synthesizer recommends a Vercel-deployed Next.js front-end orchestrated by LangGraph, using Anthropic Claude Sonnet 4.5 as the spec-generation LLM and pgvector (Supabase Postgres) for retrieval. This pairing was selected from the M4 Pareto frontier (AV.01 dominates AV.02..AV.05 across cost/latency/availability) because it satisfies the 3-second p95 user-facing budget (interface_specs chain AUTHORING_SPEC_EMIT sums 2600 ms) while keeping the 100% citation-floor (NFR.01) achievable through grounded-retrieval gating. The recommendation carries 14 high-RPN residual risks from M8.b that ride alongside the architecture; each is mapped to a landed control surfaced via M5 form_function_map.

mermaid_diagrams.sequence:
sequenceDiagram
    participant F as Founder
    participant UI as Next.js (Vercel)
    participant LG as LangGraph orch
    participant LLM as Anthropic Sonnet 4.5
    participant PG as pgvector
    F->>UI: submit idea
    UI->>LG: AuthenticatedSession (IF.01, p95 500ms)
    LG->>PG: retrieve KB chunks (IF.02, p95 600ms)

    LG->>LLM: spec gen with grounded context (IF.03, p95 1200ms)
    LLM-->>LG: draft spec
    LG->>UI: spec stream (IF.04, p95 300ms)
    UI-->>F: spec ready (sum 2600ms <= 3000ms NFR)

flowchart TB

    DN02[DN.02 Vector Store] -.winner.-> A2[pgvector]
    DN03[DN.03 Orchestration] -.winner.-> A3[LangGraph]

    A1 --> AV01{{AV.01 Recommended Architecture}}

    A3 --> AV01

pareto_frontier.name: Claude Sonnet 4.5 + pgvector + LangGraph + Vercel
pareto_frontier.summary: Recommended. Managed LLM (Anthropic) + co-located vector store (Supabase pgvector) + graph orchestration + edge-deployed Next.js. Highest tail-latency headroom; lowest ops surface area for a 1-engineer portfolio project.

cost.derivation: cost = LLM_in_per_token * tokens_per_session * sessions_per_day * 30 + pgvector_ru/hr * 730 + Vercel_pro_seat

latency.derivation: sum of IF.01..IF.04 p95 latencies along chain AUTHORING_SPEC_EMIT (interface_specs.chain_budgets)

availability.derivation: product of per-IF availabilities (Anthropic 99.95 * pgvector 99.99 * Vercel 99.99) with conservative rounding

pareto_frontier.name: GPT-4 Turbo + Pinecone + LangChain Chains + Cloud Run
pareto_frontier.summary: Rejected: same chain depth but +18% cost (Pinecone $/RU + Cloud Run cold-start premium) and +8% p95 (LangChain Chains lacks LangGraph parallelism). No availability win.

cost.derivation: Same formula as AV.01 with GPT-4 Turbo per-token + Pinecone $/RU + Cloud Run sustained price.

latency.derivation: AV.01 sum + 210 ms LangChain serial-orchestration overhead (estimated from M4 DN.03 scoring).

pareto_frontier.name: OpenRouter federated + Weaviate self-hosted + Custom orch + Fly.io
pareto_frontier.summary: Rejected: lowest LLM unit cost but +120% ops surface (Weaviate cluster + custom orchestrator + Fly.io machine mgmt). Failed PC.1 (non-invasiveness) trade-off vs solo-engineer portfolio scope.

cost.derivation: cost = OpenRouter blended/token * tokens + Weaviate node hours * 2 nodes + Fly.io machine hours; ignores ops time.

latency.derivation: AV.01 sum + 500 ms federation handoff + custom-orch coordination.

availability.derivation: Lower per-IF availabilities multiplied (federated LLM + self-hosted vector + Fly.io machines).

decisions.claim: Choose LLM provider for spec generation (F.2)

alternatives.name: OpenAI GPT-4 Turbo
alternatives.reason_rejected: +18% per-token cost; weaker citation-grounding for NFR.01.

alternatives.name: OpenRouter federated
alternatives.reason_rejected: +500ms federation handoff breaks AUTHORING_SPEC_EMIT chain budget.

decisions.rationale: Sonnet 4.5 has the strongest grounded-retrieval adherence in our test corpus (KB-8 atlas anthropic#latency-prior p95=1100ms) and cleanest 200k context window for the founder intake context.

alternatives.name: Pinecone managed
alternatives.reason_rejected: $/RU pricing inflates cost at our QPS; second network hop adds 80ms.

alternatives.name: Weaviate self-hosted
alternatives.reason_rejected: Adds 2-node cluster ops surface; no portfolio-justifiable win.

decisions.rationale: Co-locating vectors with relational state in a single Supabase Postgres collapses two network hops into one. KB-8 atlas supabase#availability-prior 99.99 satisfies NFR.04.

decisions.claim: Choose orchestration runtime for the F.2..F.7 spec-emit chain

alternatives.name: LangChain Chains
alternatives.reason_rejected: Serial composition adds ~210ms vs LangGraph parallelism.

alternatives.name: Custom orchestrator
alternatives.reason_rejected: Build cost outweighs portfolio scope; no measurable benefit.

decisions.rationale: LangGraph allows DN.03-A: parallel dispatch of recommendation + traceback agents within a single state-machine, hitting EC15 founder-intake budget (2000ms).

decisions.claim: Choose deployment target for the Next.js app tier

alternatives.name: Cloud Run
alternatives.reason_rejected: Cold-start adds 200-400ms tail; no portfolio-grade SSR optimizations.

alternatives.name: Fly.io
alternatives.reason_rejected: Machine-mgmt ops surface unjustified for solo-engineer scope.

decisions.rationale: Vercel removes ops surface entirely (PC.1) and provides Next.js-native streaming for EC16 chunk cadence (50ms).

risks.title: Spec hallucinates citations not in retrieved corpus
risks.mitigation: Grounded-retrieval gate (FR.02) + 100% citation-floor NFR.01; audit FR.10 logs every emit.

risks.title: pgvector retrieval miss bypasses grounding gate
risks.mitigation: Hybrid lexical + dense retrieval; reject empty-result generation (FR.07).

risks.title: LangGraph node failure leaves orphan partial state
risks.mitigation: Idempotent node design + replay from checkpoint; circuit-breaker per IF.

risks.title: Vercel function timeout on long spec-emit chains
risks.mitigation: Stream-by-section emission; segment chain at 8s boundary; backoff on 504.

empirical_priors.quote: Sonnet 4.5 p50=550ms p95=1100ms p99=1900ms at 8k-tok context (atlas observed window).
Sonnet 4.5 input $3/M tokens, output $15/M tokens (atlas pricing snapshot).
Supabase Postgres reports 99.99 monthly uptime SLA at the Pro tier.
pgvector ANN p95 ~30ms at 1M vector index size (atlas estimate).
LangGraph parallel-dispatch sustains 50 ops/s/agent in our benchmark suite.
Vercel Edge p95 TTFB ~50ms in our region; serverless cold-start <300ms p95.
Vercel reports 99.99 monthly uptime SLA on Pro plan.

flags.failure_mode: Spec generation hallucinates citations unsupported by DOCUMENTATION SOURCES
flags.open_residual_risk: Detectability remains imperfect — silent hallucinations within retrieved corpus still possible until SR-CORNELL validator agent ships.

flags.failure_mode: Approval fires without compliance policy check
Adapter exceeds non-invasive overhead budget on customer system
flags.open_residual_risk: Sampled-vs-full-fidelity ingest tradeoff (fmea_early.FM.04.M2) NOT yet committed — customer overhead under burst load still possible.

flags.failure_mode: Recommendation cadence exceeds RECOMMENDATION_CADENCE_MIN (60 min, est.)
flags.open_residual_risk: RECOMMENDATION_CADENCE_MIN not yet promoted to NFR (FM.06.M2 deferred) — no contract-level enforcement.

flags.failure_mode: Traceback emits recommendation without complete citation chain
CLI bundle (DE.06) ships with secrets embedded
Recommendation loop (DE.10 → F.2) creates specification drift
LLM provider outage (DE.13 unavailable) halts spec generation
Primary/fallback LLM split-brain: divergent specs on transient primary degradation
flags.open_residual_risk: No request-level provider stickiness; circuit-breaker (interface_specs IF.01..IF.10 error_rate_threshold_pct=25) trips at session granularity but does not pin a single founder session to one provider. Recommended next-cycle ADR: pin (founder_id, project_id) → provider for session lifetime.

flags.failure_mode: Event-bus cascade failure: IF.06 streaming backlog starves IF.10 feedback loop
flags.open_residual_risk: No isolation between IF.06 and IF.10 runtimes in chosen LangGraph topology. ADR candidate: separate consumer pool for streaming vs in-process interfaces, or migrate IF.06 to dedicated worker process.

flags.failure_mode: pgvector index drift: HNSW recall degrades silently after corpus growth
flags.open_residual_risk: No periodic recall benchmark scheduled. ADR candidate: add weekly recall@k probe over canonical query set; trigger HNSW rebuild when recall drops below 0.9.

flags.failure_mode: Vercel cold-start tax violates IF.06 streaming SLA on FR.06 adapter
flags.open_residual_risk: Vercel free/pro tier cold-start latency is non-contractual. ADR candidate: warm FR.06 via scheduled ping or migrate FR.06 to long-running Cloud Run (matches identity-api pattern in monorepo recommendation).

next_steps: Wire telemetry probe stack to capture per-IF p95 latencies in production for prior-refresh.
Promote 4 mitigation controls (FR.02, FR.07, FR.10, IF.04 stream) into the test plan.
Backfill kb_chunk_ids[] with real pgvector UUIDs once corpus is ingested at full scale.
Re-run synthesis quarterly; recompute inputs_hash to detect drift in upstream artifacts.
